response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer with decades of experience across web, mobile, and some systems programming, but the role demands deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. The low-latency DLang work at Sociomantic and C/C++ work at National Instruments show systems aptitude, but there is no evidence of GPU architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, SIMD/MIMD work, or silicon-level performance engineering required for a Principal Performance Architect.
strengths: Extensive engineering experience (25+ years) easily exceeding the years-of-experience bar
Low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic
C/C++ background at National Instruments on measurement tooling used by hardware engineers
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator experience whatsoever
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or performance-architecture background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, misaligned with a hardware performance principal IC role

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and accelerator expertise
